_notes: WP-216 / EC-248 — curated, human-reviewed hero ability markup map. Consumed by apply-hero-ability-markers.mjs, which appends [keyword:rescue:N] / [keyword:reveal] / [keyword:reveal:N] tokens to the specified hero ability lines in data/cards/*.json.

Keys: setAbbr -> array of { heroSlug, cardSlug, abilityIndex, markupToken }. abilityIndex is 0-based into card.abilities[]. Valid markupToken values: '[keyword:rescue:1]', '[keyword:reveal]', '[keyword:reveal:2]' (D-21601 closed set).

Curation discipline: a line is marked ONLY when it unambiguously reduces to a single-bystander rescue or a reveal-draw with an extractable cost threshold of 2. Condition-gated lines ([hc:X]:, [team:X]:) are included — the executor evaluates conditions already. Timing-gated lines (Fight:, Ambush:, Heist:, [keyword:Ambush], [keyword:Excessive Violence], [keyword:Excessive Kindness], [keyword:Focus]) are deferred per D-21602/D-21603.

Web-Shooters (core/spider-man) is NOT included — WP-215 already applied rescue:1 and reveal markup to those two lines. The idempotence guard would skip them, but we omit them from the map to keep it authoritative for WP-216 additions only.

Amendment (inline, EC-248 fold): AC #11 and EC §After Completing originally asserted grep count=1 for rescue:1 in core.json. Execution confirmed core/black-widow/mission-accomplished is an in-scope candidate (discovered by --propose). Count updated to 2 (web-shooters from WP-215 + mission-accomplished from WP-216). Corrected in WP-216 AC #11 and EC-248 §After Completing as part of Commit A.

_deferred.abilityText: Heist: Rescue a Bystander.
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — onFight-timed rescue: 'Heist:' is a custom timing prefix analogous to 'Fight:'; the onFight timing is not wired to executeHeroEffects.

_deferred.abilityText: Reveal the top card of your deck. If it costs 2 or more, draw it. Otherwise, discard it or put it back.
_deferred.reason: reveal-min-or-discard-choose: Inverted cost threshold (draw if cost ≥ N) with a player-choice fallback (discard OR put it back) on the low branch. Requires both a reveal-min-style draw AND pendingHeroChoice for the fallback — a compound not expressible with current executors.

_deferred.abilityText: [keyword:Focus] 4[icon:recruit] [icon:5] Rescue a Bystander, then you may KO a card from your hand or discard pile.
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — keyword timing prefix: not wired to executeHeroEffects; also carries per-turn-use-count limit and compound rescue+KO effect

_deferred.abilityText: [team:heroes-of-wakanda] [keyword:Ambush]: Rescue a Bystander.
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — keyword timing prefix: not wired to executeHeroEffects

_deferred.abilityText: [keyword:Excessive Violence]: Rescue a Bystander.
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — onFight-timed rescue: '[keyword:Excessive Violence]:' is a card-ability keyword timing prefix analogous to '[keyword:Ambush]:'; executor dispatch for this keyword timing is not wired to executeHeroEffects.

_deferred.abilityText: Reveal the top card of your deck. If it costs 0[icon:recruit] or has no team icon, draw it.
_deferred.reason: reveal-recruit-zero-or-no-team-draw: Compound OR condition: draw if recruit-cost = 0 OR the card has no team icon. Requires evaluating recruit cost (not VP cost) and team-icon presence — neither is in the current executor design.

_deferred.abilityText: [hc:strength]: Reveal the top card of your deck. KO it or [keyword:Teleport] it.
_deferred.reason: reveal-ko-or-teleport: Player choice: KO the card OR Teleport it. [keyword:Teleport] is a movement mechanic (relocate card to a different zone/location) not in the current executor family.

_deferred.abilityText: Reveal the top card of your deck. Discard it or put it back.
_deferred.reason: reveal-discard-choose: Player must choose to discard OR put the card back. No cost evaluation. No draw. Requires G.pendingHeroChoice routing with a discard-or-return choice object (no attack grant, unlike reveal-attack-choose).

_deferred.abilityText: [keyword:Focus 2][icon:recruit]  [icon:5] Rescue a Bystander. You may only use this ability up to four times this turn.
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — [keyword:Focus 2] modifies how the ability triggers (Focus token mechanic); the Focus condition is not in the hero condition evaluation pipeline. Also carries a per-turn-use-count limit not expressible in the current executor.

_deferred.abilityText: [keyword:Patrol the Rooftops]: If it's empty, reveal the top card of your deck. You may KO it.
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — keyword timing prefix: not wired to executeHeroEffects; also 'You may KO it' is optional, unlike unconditional reveal-ko

_deferred.abilityText: [hc:tech]: If a Bystander is KO'd this way, you may also rescue that Bystander instead of putting it in the KO pile.
_deferred.reason: KO-event response rescue: rescue is triggered by a prior KO in the same ability chain, not as a standalone action. Requires event-response plumbing not present in the current hero effects executor.

_deferred.abilityText: [keyword:Moonlight]: Whenever you defeat a Vilain or Mastermind this turn, you may rescue a Bystander or gain a Hero from the HQ or Sidekick Deck whose cost is less than that Enemy's [icon:attack].
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — fight-win trigger: not wired to executeHeroEffects

_deferred.abilityText: Once this turn, you may fight the top card of the Bystander Deck as if it were a 4[icon:attack] “Darkhold Demon” Vilan with “Fight: KO up to two of your Heroes. Rescue this card as a Bystander.”
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — fight-win trigger: not wired to executeHeroEffects; non-standard combat against Bystander Deck stack

_deferred.abilityText: [keyword:Excessive Kindness]: Rescue a Bystander.
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — onFight-timed rescue: '[keyword:Excessive Kindness]:' is a card-ability keyword timing prefix analogous to '[keyword:Ambush]:'; executor dispatch for this keyword timing is not wired to executeHeroEffects.

_deferred.abilityText: [hc:instinct]: [keyword:Investigate] the Bystander Stack for a Bystander and rescue it.
_deferred.reason: [keyword:Investigate] searches the full Bystander Stack (not just the top card); requires stack-search logic not in the current executor family.

_deferred.abilityText: Reveal the top card of your deck. If it's [hc:instinct] or [hc:tech], draw it.
_deferred.reason: reveal-class-conditional-draw: Draw conditional on the revealed card's hero class. No cost evaluation.

_deferred.abilityText: Whenever you defeat a Villain on the Rooftops this turn, rescue Bystanders equal to that Villain's printed [icon:piercing].
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — fight-win trigger: not wired to executeHeroEffects; also scales to defeated villain's piercing value

_deferred.abilityText: Reveal the top card of your deck. If that card has an [icon:attack] icon, draw it.
_deferred.reason: reveal-icon-conditional-draw: Draw conditional on the revealed card having a specific icon ([icon:attack] or [icon:recruit]). No cost evaluation.

_deferred.abilityText: Reveal the top card of your deck. If that card has a [icon:recruit] icon, draw it.
_deferred.reason: reveal-icon-conditional-draw: Draw conditional on the revealed card having a specific icon ([icon:attack] or [icon:recruit]). No cost evaluation.

_deferred.abilityText: Reveal the top card of your deck. If it costs 1 [icon:vp] or 2 [icon:vp], you get +2[icon:attack].
_deferred.reason: reveal-cost-range-attack: Attack bonus if the revealed card's cost falls in a specific range (cost ∈ {1, 2}) — not a simple ≤ threshold. A new cost-range parameter format would be required.

_deferred.abilityText: Reveal the top card of your deck. Discard it or put it back.
_deferred.reason: reveal-discard-choose: Player must choose to discard OR put the card back. No cost evaluation. No draw. Requires G.pendingHeroChoice routing with a discard-or-return choice object (no attack grant, unlike reveal-attack-choose).

_deferred.abilityText: Choose a Hero Class. ([hc:strength],[hc:instinct],[hc:covert],[hc:tech] or [hc:ranged]) Reveal the top card of your deck. If it's the Hero Class you named, draw it. Otherwise, put it back on the top or bottom.
_deferred.reason: reveal-player-named-class-draw: Player declares a hero class at activation time (before the reveal); draw if the revealed card matches the declared class. Requires ability-activation flow changes not present in the current design.

_deferred.abilityText: Whenever you defeat a Villain or Mastermind this turn, draw a card and rescue a Bystander.
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — fight-win trigger: not wired to executeHeroEffects

_deferred.abilityText: Reveal the top card of your deck. You may KO it.
_deferred.reason: reveal-optional-ko: 'You may KO it' — the player may choose not to KO (unlike the unconditional reveal-ko).

_deferred.abilityText: Reveal the top card of your deck, then put it back on top of your deck or into your discard pile. If that card was an [hc:instinct] Ally, you get +2[icon:attack].
_deferred.reason: reveal-conditional-discard-or-return compound: Multi-branch reveals where the outcome depends on class, team, or card type, and the player has a choice on at least one branch.

_deferred.abilityText: Reveal the top card of your deck. If it's a [team:brotherhood] Ally, you may draw it. Otherwise, you may KO it.
_deferred.reason: reveal-conditional-discard-or-return compound: Multi-branch reveals where the outcome depends on class, team, or card type, and the player has a choice on at least one branch.

_deferred.abilityText: [keyword:Excessive Violence]: Reveal the top card of your deck. If it costs 0, KO it and you may repeat this process.
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — keyword timing prefix: not wired to executeHeroEffects; also loop mechanic requires new infrastructure even if timing resolved

_deferred.abilityText: [keyword:Excessive Violence]: Reveal the top card of your deck. You may KO it.
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — keyword timing prefix: not wired to executeHeroEffects

_deferred.abilityText: [hc:ranged]: Reveal the top card of your deck. If it costs 0, discard it and you get +2[icon:attack].
_deferred.reason: reveal-discard-attack: Similar to reveal-ko-attack but discards the card instead of KO-ing it when cost = 0. Discard vs. KO is a meaningful gameplay difference; a reveal-discard-attack executor (or a mode parameter on reveal-ko-attack) would be needed.

_deferred.abilityText: [keyword:Excessive Violence]: “Rescue“ a Bystander.
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — keyword timing prefix: not wired to executeHeroEffects

_deferred.abilityText: [keyword:Indigestion]: “Rescue“ a Bystander.
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — keyword timing prefix: not wired to executeHeroEffects

_deferred.abilityText: [keyword:Indigestion]: “Rescue“ two Bystanders.
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — keyword timing prefix: not wired to executeHeroEffects

_deferred.abilityText: [hc:instinct][keyword:Soulbind a Bystander or Villain]: You get +2[icon:attack]. If it's Special Bystander, you may do its Rescue effect.
_deferred.reason: Soulbind keyword captures a bystander/villain face-down; "do its Rescue effect" requires inline execution of a Special Bystander's embedded rescue effect text — not expressible in the current executor family.

_deferred.abilityText: Whenever you recruit a Hero that costs 5 or more this turn, reveal the top card of your deck and you may KO it.
_deferred.reason: D-21602 — recruit-event trigger: not wired to executeHeroEffects
